rulings.Case Title: J.R. MARKETING, LLC., A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al VS. HARTFORD CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Application To File Materials Under Seal
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 16, 2010, line 7, PLAINTIFF J.R. MARKETING, LLC., JANE RATTO, ROBERT RATTO, PENELOPE KANE, LENORE DEMARTINIS, and, GERMAIN DEMARTINIS Application to File Materials Under Seal. Motion denied without prejudice. Failure to comply with CRC 2.550(d). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: J.R. MARKETING, LLC., A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al VS. HARTFORD CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Hartford Casualty Insurance Company To Dissolve Or Modify The "Enforcement Order" Entered By Sep-27-06
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 16, 2010, line 7, CROSS COMPLAINANT HARTFORD CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY Motion by Hartford Casualty Insurance Company to Dissolve or Modify the "Enforcement Order" Entered by September 27, 2006. Motion denied. Long vs. Century Indemnity Co. (2008) 163 Cal.App. 4th 1460 is distinguishable in that the insurer provided a defense and did not breach its duty to defend. Compulink Management Center, Inc. vs. St. Paul Fire and Marine Insurance Co. (2008) 169 Cal.App. 4th 289 is similarly distinguishable and did not address the situation raised in the instant action. Where in the insurer breaches its duty to defend it forfeits the protection of Civil Code section 2860. (See Intergulf Dev't Co. vs. Superior Court (2010) Daily Journal D.A.R. 4406) Defendant's remedy is to pursue the reimbursement claim. No authority supports the posting of a bond. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: J.R. MARKETING, LLC., A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al VS. HARTFORD CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant And Cross-Complainant Hartford Casualty Insurance Company'S Application To Seal Certain Documents Re: Hartford Casualty Insurance Companys' Motion To Dissolve And/Or Modify "Enforcement Order" Entered September 27, 2006
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 16, 2010, line 7, Defendant and Cross-Complainant Hartford Casualty Insurance Company's Application to Seal Certain Documents Re: Hartford Casualty Insurance Company's Motion to Dissolve and/or Modify "Enforcement Order" Entered September 27, 2006. As drafted, application denied without prejudice. Failure to comply with CRC 2.550 and 2.551. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: 1054 KEARNY,LLC,DBA HEAVEN MINI-THEATER VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To File X-Complaint And Vacate Trial Date
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 16, 2010, line 1, CROSS DEFENDANT ALYCE DAVIS KELLEY CRAFT, Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint and Vacate Trial Date. Grant motion for leave to file amended cross-complaint. Notice of Motion to vacate trial date is off calendar, matter to be re-noticed in Department 206. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DOUGLAS YEP VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgemnt On The Pleadings;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 16, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings. Motion granted with leave to amend for plaintiff to plead compliance with the Government Claims Act. Plaintiff's "claims," as pleaded, fail to comply with Government Code section 910. The series of "claims" that plaintiff presented over the course of years do not constitute substantial compliance. Schaefer Dixon Associates vs. Santa Ana Watershed Project Authority (1996) 48 Cal.App.4th 524. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MANDANA FARHANG VS. JOSE R ALLEN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 16, 2010, line 3, DEFENDANTS JOSE ALLEN, SKADDEN, ARPS SLATE MEAGHER & FLOM LLP DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Off Calendar. Consolidated with Case No. 492063. Matter to be re-noticed in Department 301. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SCOTT MAHALICK VS. ENTERCOM SAN FRANCISCO LLC A DELAWARE LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion And Motion To Stay Or Dismiss
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 16, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANTS ENTERCOM COMMUNICATIONS CORP., ENTERCOM SAN FRANCISCO LLC, ENTERCOM SEATTLE LLC, and, DWIGHT WALKER Motion to Stay or Dismiss. Motion granted in part. This action is stayed pending the outcome of the earlier filed action in United States District Court for the District of Oregon entitled Entercom, et al vs. Scott Mahalick, Case number CV 09-1050 HA. =(302/CWW)

Set for hearing on Friday, April 16, 2010, line 8, IDENTURE TRUSTEE POLICYHOLDERS MOTION TO INTERVENE. Motion denied. ITP's interest in the instant litigation is not direct, and intervention threatens the rights of the original parties to conduct the action on their own terms. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO JUDO INSTITUTE VS. GENE ROYALE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Order To Show Cause To Defendants Re: Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 16, 2010, line 5, Plaintiff's Order to Show Cause to Defendants Re: Preliminary Injunction. Granted. No opposition filed. Bond requirement of CCP 529 waived. See Smith vs. Adventist Health System/West (2010) 182 Cal.App.4th 729 (2010 WL 724698). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: BANK OF NEW YORK AS TRUSTEE FOR THE CERTIFICATE VS. MILDRED PHILLIPS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 16, 2010, line 6, DEFENDANT MILDRED PHILLIPS DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer overruled. Plaintiff pleads a cause of action for an unlawful detainer and defendant raises issues that should be asserted in a motion for summary judgment. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, AS TRUSTEE VS. MICHELE TCHIKOVANI et al
